headline: Super vs Gemini: Computer‑Use Agents for Real Work

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Gemini. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost.
site_brief.title: Super vs Gemini

summary: A fair, editorial comparison of Super and Google Gemini for people evaluating personal AI agents that can operate computers. Gemini has recently added agentic computer‑use across devices and platforms, while Super is purpose‑built for repeated computer‑use workflows with a reusable computer‑use cache that can lower the cost and friction of running the same tasks again and again. This page contrasts where each shines and why Super is the sharper alternative for hands‑on automation.
